name: education_communications_outage_forces_kestrel_jus_mer
premise: A Education district emergency desk is in a fast-moving incident involving education communications outage forces kestrel jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases student safety, continuity, and fairness. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Hector Chen
agents.identity: I am Hector Chen, accountable for a defensible call in Education when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Soren Navarro
agents.identity: I am Soren Navarro, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Education.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Hector Chen must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Hector Chen do?
